original: Former astronaut Kathy Sullivan is the first woman to reach deepest point of the ocean - Challenger Deep in the Marianas Trench. Formerly the first American woman to spacewalk.
World's most powerful supercomputer, Summit, can process 200,000 trillion calculations per second, launched at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Tennessee, by IBM and Nvidia
Nippon Airways announces that painting eyeballs on Jets cut bird collisions by 20%
US prisoner Richard Anthony Jones freed after 17 years in Roeland Park, Kansas when his lookalike discovered
"Ghostbusters", American supernatural comedy film, directed and produced byIvan Reitman, starringBill Murray,Dan Aykroyd, Harold Ramis, and Ernie Hudson premieres
"Gremlins", American comedy horror film, is released
World's largest flower blooms in the NY Botanical Garden, 12-foot calla lily
Naomi James, the first woman to sail solo around the world via Cape Horn, and the second woman ever, arrives back in Dartmouth England after 272 days
"Ms Marvel" TV miniseries premieres on Disney+, with Disney's first on-screen Muslim superhero story, starring Iman Vellani
Discovery of element 93, neptunium, is announced
Bruce McCandless II, American aviator, electrical engineer and astronaut who made the 1st untethered space walk (STS 41B, STS-31), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 2017)
Spanish flagship galleon San José is sunk during a battle just off the coast of  Cartagena, Columbia carrying a cargo of gold, silver and emerald valued today in billions

Gert Terblanche, a local school boy, discovers fossils of an unknown 'robust-type' human ancestor, later named Paranthropus robustus by Robert Broom, at Kromdraai, Blaauwbank River Valley in South Africa
"The Source" goes online, the first public computer information service
National Geographic announces it is officially recognizing the South Ocean as the world's fifth ocean
USSR launches Venera 9 for Venus landing
Giovanni Domenico Cassini, Italian-French mathematician, astronomer and engineer who discovered four of Saturn's moons, born in Perinaldo, Republic of Genova (d. 1712)
Transit of Venus occurs between Earth and the Sun
Nova Aquilae is discovered, the brightest nova since Kepler's nova of 1604
Ives W. McGaffey of Chicago patents the first vacuum cleaner, calling it a "sweeping machine"
51st National Spelling Bee: Peg McCarthy wins spelling deification
First section of the elevated High Line park, built on an old railway opens in New York from Gansevoort Street to 20th Street
X-15 makes 1st unpowered flight, from a B-52 at 11,500 m
Herman Hollerith receives a patent for his punch card calculator

Topeka, Kansas is devastated by a tornado that registers as an "F5" on the Fujita Scale: the first to exceed US$100 million in damages. Sixteen people are killed, hundreds more injured, and thousands of homes damaged or destroyed.
New Zealand's Labour government legislates against nuclear weapons and nuclear powered vessels, the only nation to legislate against nuclear power
Patrick Kanescores a playoff hat-trick against the Los Angeles Kings in Game 5 of the Western Conference Finals
Athletics' first basemanJimmie Foxxhits 3 consecutive home runs as Philadelphia outscores New York Yankees, 14 - 10; 4 straight HRs after Foxx had homered last time up the previous day
Boston Red Sox rout St Louis Browns, 29-4 at Fenway Park; set 6 MLB records including runs scored and most total bases, 60
MLB California Angels pitcherNolan Ryannotches his 4th career 19-strikeout game, in 10-inning, 2-1 win over visiting Toronto Blue Jays
Former NY Jet Mark Gastineau wins 1st pro boxing fight in 12 seconds
"Trading Places", American comedy film, is released
Nancy Sinatra, American singer ("These Boots Are Made for Walkin'"; "Something Stupid"), and daughter of Frank Sinatra, born in Jersey City, New Jersey
Scott Adams, American cartoonist (Dilbert) and author, born in Windham, New York
NFL and AFL announce merger plans to form one league with two conferences (NFC and AFC) starting in 1970
British-CanadianLennox Lewisretains boxing's WBC Heavyweight title with eighth-round knockout of AmericanMike Tysonat The Pyramid Arena in Memphis, Tennessee
Lockdowns for COVID-19 in Europe saved 3 million lives according to study by Imperial College London
The Philippines says children won't go back to school until a vaccine is found for COVID-19
Downed US Air Force pilot Captain Scott O'Grady is rescued by marines in Bosnia
John Rudder becomes the first African American commissioned officer in the US Marine Corps
Robert Morris, 1st successful African American lawyer in the US and Abolitionist, born in Salem, Massachusetts (d. 1882)
Newcastle, New South Wales, Australia is hit by the State's worst storms and flooding in 30 years resulting in the death of nine people and the grounding of trade ship, the MV Pasha Bulker
7.8 earthquake strikes North Bolivia
1st US-to-Australia flight lands (Sir Charles Kingford)
East German sprinter Marlies Göhr sets world record for 100m (10.81) in Berlin, Germany
Butch Reynolds, American athlete who held 400m WR 43.29s 1988-99; Olympic gold 4x400m relay, silver 400m 1988, born in Akron, Ohio
MLB Draft: Kansas City Athletics A's select Arizona State University outfielder Rick Monday #1; inaugural MLB Draft
MLB Brooklyn Dodgers option pitcher (and future Baseball Hall of Fame manager)Tommy Lasordato make room on roster for future Hall of Famer, pitcherSandy Koufax
MLB legendMickey Mantlegives his farewell retirement speech during "Mickey Mantle Day" at Yankee Stadium as 60,096 fans see #7 retired
Ray Bourqueplays the final game in his NHL career
Warren Schutte is 1st non American to win NCAA Division 1 golf title
Test Cricket debut of famous West Indian spin twins Alf Valentine andSonny Ramadhinin 1st Test v England at Manchester; Valentine takes 8-104 on Day 1 but WI lose by 202 runs
Bing Crosbyrecords "Silent Night"
GuitaristBrian Jonesis asked to leavethe Rolling Stones, replaced by Mick Taylor
Rolling Stones release "Jumpin' Jack Flash"
Test Cricket debut ofTony Greig, v Australia at Old Trafford (57/62)
Jazz legendOscar Petersonforced to cancel his appearance at Carnegie Hall all-star performance (held in his honor), due to illness
"The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour" variety show last airs on CBS-TV
Keenan Ivory Wayans, American comedian (In Living Color), born in New York City
Robert Preston [Meservey], American stage and screen actor and singer (The Music Man; Mame; The Last Starfighter), born in Newton, Massachusetts (d. 1987)
